medications: Cetirizine as needed during allergy season
health.mental_health: Since the promotion, he’s been dealing with persistent anxiety about managing others, trouble sleeping before Monday pipeline meetings, and occasional irritability; he’s considering therapy but hasn’t told most coworkers.
recent_visits: Primary care visit (annual physical; discussed sleep and stress) - 6 weeks ago
Pulmonology follow-up for asthma control - 4 months ago
Telehealth psychiatry/med management appointment for ADHD medication adjustment - 2 months ago

finance.debt: About $22,000 in student loans; $7,500 on a credit card carried after a slow sales month and moving costs. He’s also on a payment plan for last year’s federal taxes after under-withholding on commissions.
recent_transactions: Paid $2,300 for apartment renewal deposit and moving-related fees
Spent ~$1,150 on a new laptop/phone upgrade for work
Paid $480 for ADHD telehealth/medication visit not fully covered by insurance

relationships: Priya Banda (older sister in Austin) — checks in weekly; he downplays how overwhelmed he feels
Maya Patel (girlfriend, 24, nurse in Plano) — supportive but frustrated by his unpredictable hours
Jordan McCall (best friend from college in Dallas) — confidant about work stress and money worries
Devon Reyes (former peer, now direct report) — tension since the promotion
social.social_media_activity: Active on Instagram and TikTok for real estate content (listing tours, neighborhood reels) while keeping personal posts minimal; LinkedIn updates about the promotion but avoids mentioning struggles.

Discussing ADHD medication side effects and fear of coworkers finding out
Arguments with Maya about missed dinners and him answering client calls late at night
Confiding to Jordan about credit card balance and the IRS payment plan

schedule.work_hours: Typically 9:00am–7:00pm weekdays, with client showings 1–5 hours on most Saturdays; often responds to messages until 10:30pm.
regular_appointments: Monday 8:15am pipeline/forecast meeting with the team
Wednesday 6:30pm gym session with a trainer (when not canceled for showings)
Every other Friday 4:00pm asthma inhaler refill pickup
upcoming_events: Brokerage leadership workshop in Uptown Dallas - next Thursday 1:00pm–5:00pm
Two listing presentations in Plano and Richardson - Saturday morning
Dinner with Maya’s friends (he already warned he might be late) - next Sunday 7:00pm
schedule.daily_routine: Coffee and email triage in the morning, office check-in and team coordination midday, showings/listing prep late afternoon, then follow-ups and contract paperwork at night; sleep is inconsistent when deals are closing.

professional.company: Lone Star Metro Realty Group (mid-size Dallas brokerage with a residential sales team)
projects: Managing a 6-agent team’s lead routing and weekly performance tracking
Overseeing a new CRM rollout and training sessions for agents
Handling a high-value listing near Lakewood with demanding sellers
Building a first-time homebuyer workshop series for spring leads
professional.performance_issues: He’s missing some internal deadlines (CRM notes, follow-up audits) and has had two tense client escalations because he delegated tasks without clear instructions. He worries his ADHD makes him look disorganized in management and he’s quietly tracking mistakes to avoid bringing them up to his broker.
workplace_relationships: Broker/manager (Elaine Harper) — supportive but expects metrics; has started asking for weekly accountability reports
Direct report (Devon Reyes) — resents being managed by a former peer and challenges his decisions in meetings
Top producer (Kelsey Nguyen) — helpful mentor but blunt about his need to set boundaries
Office admin (Maribel Santos) — keeps him on track with paperwork; he relies on her reminders more than he admits

hobbies: Pickup basketball at a rec center in North Dallas
Trying new coffee shops and filming short neighborhood clips for content
Podcasts about sales, real estate investing, and ADHD productivity
Weekend drives to explore suburbs (Frisco, McKinney) for market familiarity
habits: Uses a nicotine vape during high-stress days (trying to quit; not public)
Relies on caffeine to push through late paperwork
Time-blocking and reminder apps to manage ADHD, but often abandons systems when overwhelmed
Occasional late-night fast food after showings, especially during closing weeks
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a one-bedroom apartment in the Knox-Henderson area; moved recently to be closer to the office and showings, which increased expenses during a slower commission month.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly eats quick, high-protein meals (chicken bowls, meal kits when he remembers); tries to limit dairy when asthma flares and avoids heavy foods before showings.
